brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +31.0% since Feb 27 — CEASEFIRE: Brent crashes 14-16% to ~$93 (Apr 8); Pakistan-brokered two-week pause; largest single-day plunge of war; still +31% above pre-war $71
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 7 close: ~$109.27/bbl. Apr 8: Brent plunges 14-16% to ~$91.70-$94.22 overnight on ceasefire news — one of the largest single-day drops in history. Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7, 88 minutes before Trump's 8 PM deadline. Iran accepted within the hour. Oil remains 31% above pre-war levels. Ceasefire conditional on Hormuz reopening; Iran's 'coordinated safe passage' narrower than Trump's 'complete, immediate' demand. Islamabad Accords talks begin Apr 10. Gold continues rising ($4,737) despite ceasefire — persistent market skepticism. EIA forecasts retail gas peaking $4.30/gal in April. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +44.4% since Feb 27 — CEASEFIRE: WTI crashes 15-19% to ~$96 (Apr 8); one of largest single-day plunges in history; still +44% above pre-war $66.50
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 7: WTI spiked to ~$115 on Kharg strikes. Apr 8: WTI plunges 15-19% to ~$95-$97 on ceasefire news — one of the largest single-day drops in crude oil history. Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7. Prices remain 44% above pre-war levels. Gas $4.14/gal nationally (AAA) — lags crude by 1-2 weeks. EIA forecasts peak $4.30/gal in April — ceasefire may cap this. Gold continues rising to $4,737 despite ceasefire — persistent hedging demand signals market skepticism. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.16 / +38.9% since Feb 27 — $4.14 nationally (Apr 8 AAA) — lags crude by 1-2 weeks — EIA forecasts peak $4.30 in April — ceasefire may cap rise
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 8: $4.14/gal nationally (AAA) — $1.16 surge in 40 days. Gasoline lags crude oil by 1-2 weeks, so the overnight 15-19% crude crash may not reduce pump prices immediately. EIA forecasts retail gas peaking at $4.30/gal in April — the ceasefire may cap this. Up from $2.98 pre-war (+38.9%). California still highest at ~$5.89/gal. Oklahoma lowest ~$3.27. Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -270 pts / -3.9% — CEASEFIRE RALLY: Dow futures +1,000+ pts overnight; S&P ~6,609 (Apr 8 mid-session); VIX expected sharp decline; UBS cuts target 7,700→7,500
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 7 close: 6,616.85. Apr 8: Dow futures surged 1,000+ points overnight on ceasefire. S&P mid-session ~6,609. Ceasefire announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7, 88 min before Trump's 8 PM deadline. European airlines surged 8.9-13.6% on oil drop. XOM -6% to ~$154; CVX -5.5% to ~$190. Airlines expected +9-14% (DAL reports Q1 today). Defense faces ceasefire headwind. 10Y Treasury yields plummeting. Gold $4,737 (still rising — persistent hedging demand signals market skepticism). UBS cut S&P target from 7,700 to 7,500. 'Death cross' formed in late March. S&P still -3.9% YTD and below 200-day MA. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / UBS / Goldman Sachs.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — CEASEFIRE: Two-week pause from 8 PM ET Apr 7 — reduced operational tempo — Islamabad Accords talks begin Apr 10 — VP Vance leads US delegation
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 40: ~$800M/day (reduced from ~$1.3B on ceasefire). Two-week ceasefire announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7, brokered by Pakistan's PM Sharif and Field Marshal Munir. Iran accepted within the hour. Operations at reduced tempo but forces remain deployed: Lincoln CSG in Arabian Sea, Bush CSG transiting (ETA Apr 10-12), Tripoli ARG operational, 82nd Airborne in theater. Ceasefire conditional on Hormuz reopening. Islamabad Accords negotiations begin Apr 10. Pentagon $200B+ supplemental still faces 71% opposition. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Reuters.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (40 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$47.7B through Day 40 — CEASEFIRE — $200B supplemental: 71% oppose — Quinnipiac 37% approval — Reuters/Ipsos 36% — 61% actively disapprove — 75% oppose ground troops
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 40: ~$47.7B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. Ceasefire reduces burn rate but forces remain deployed. Quinnipiac: 37% approval. Reuters/Ipsos: 36% (down from 40%). Fox News: 59% disapproval — highest either Trump term. 61% actively disapprove of war (up 18 pts from early March). 75% oppose ground troops including 52% of Republicans. No 'rally around the flag' effect. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / Quinnipiac / Reuters / Fox News.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE IN EFFECT — 35,500+ targets through Day 39 — CENTCOM: 13,000+ struck, 155 ships sunk/damaged — Kharg Island 50+ targets Apr 7 — Israel bridge/railway campaign — ceasefire halts operations
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM/Stars and Stripes: 13,000+ targets struck, 155 Iranian ships sunk/damaged, 26 different aircraft types employed. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,500+ through Day 39. Two-week ceasefire announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7 — operations halted. Last major strikes before ceasefire: US 50+ targets on Kharg Island; Israel wide-scale bridge/railway campaign (Qom, Kashan, Tabriz-Zanjan, Karaj). Ceasefire followed post-announcement violations but stabilized by Apr 8. Islamabad Accords talks begin Apr 10. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 15 US KIA confirmed — 303+ WIA (CENTCOM Mar 28) / 520+ (Intercept) — 15 wounded at Ali Al Salem Kuwait Apr 7 — ceasefire in effect — F-15E crew fully recovered
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 15 KIA confirmed. CENTCOM DMDC: at least 303 wounded (Mar 28). The Intercept: 520+. 15 Americans wounded by Iranian drone strike at Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait on April 7 (Day 39). F-15E crew shot down April 3 fully recovered — WSO rescued by SEAL Team 6 after 36 hours evading in Zagros Mountains. A-10 also lost April 3, pilot recovered safely. Total aircraft losses: 7 airframes + 12+ MQ-9 Reaper drones. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7. Key incidents: 6 KIA Port Shuaiba Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 KIA KC-135 crash Iraq (Mar 12); 1 health-related death Kuwait. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: CENTCOM: 303+ WIA (Mar 28) — Intercept: 520+ — 15 wounded Ali Al Salem Kuwait Apr 7 — ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM: at least 303 wounded (Mar 28). The Intercept reports 520+, accusing CENTCOM of 'casualty cover-up.' 15 Americans wounded by Iranian drone strike at Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait on April 7 (Day 39) — the last major attack before ceasefire. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7. Estimated 535+ total WIA. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Reuters / Axios.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ injured — HRANA: 3,597 (1,665 civilians, 244 children) — Hengaw: 7,300+ — 334 health facilities damaged — 3.2M displaced — ceasefire in effect
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded. HRANA (Apr 6): 3,597 killed (1,665 civilians, 244 children, 1,221 military, 711 unclassified). Hengaw (Apr 2): 7,300+ (6,410 military, 890 civilians, 180 minors). Iran International: 4,700+ security forces (Mar 31). WHO (Mar 25): 1,200+ (200+ children). 3.5x gap between Hengaw and Health Ministry reflects information blackout and different counting methodologies. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. 334 health/emergency centers damaged (IRCS). 763 schools damaged. 115,193 civilian structures damaged. 3.2M displaced. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / WHO / OCHA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,422-1,500 killed (125 children) — 400+ Hezbollah fighters — 3 UN peacekeepers — Netanyahu: ceasefire EXCLUDES Lebanon — IDF three divisions south of Litani — 1M+ displaced
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli ground invasion since March 2. 1,422-1,500 killed including 125 children. 400+ Hezbollah fighters dead. 3 UN peacekeepers killed. Three IDF divisions operating south of the Litani River. Over 1 million displaced. CRITICAL: Netanyahu explicitly stated ceasefire does NOT apply to Lebanon — directly contradicting Pakistan's PM Sharif who said it covers 'Lebanon and elsewhere.' This is a critical unresolved fault line in the ceasefire framework. Hezbollah launched 24 attack waves on Apr 7 (~40 rockets hitting communities including Nahariya, 2 civilians injured), then halted attacks after ceasefire. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNICEF / UNHCR.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: UNHCR: 7.5M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M+ — Lebanon 1M+ — ceasefire may slow displacement — Islamabad talks Apr 10 — 150,000 cut off south of Litani — UNHCR Lebanon only 14% funded
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: up to 3.2 million Iranians temporarily displaced. Lebanon: over 1 million displaced. 150,000 cut off south of Litani. Two-week ceasefire may slow displacement in Iran but Lebanon excluded per Netanyahu. Islamabad Accords talks begin April 10. UNHCR Lebanon response only 14% funded against $472M requirement. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — Hormuz 'coordinated safe passage' announced — traffic dropped 94% from peacetime — ceasefire may gradually restore — 29 commercial ships attacked — 11 seafarers killed
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Ceasefire announced 8 PM ET Apr 7 — Hormuz 'coordinated safe passage' announced but narrower than full reopening. Ship traffic dropped 94% from peacetime levels. Iran attacked 29 commercial ships (13 tankers) killing 11 seafarers. Mines in shipping lanes require clearance (LCS ships USS Canberra, Tulsa, Santa Barbara based in Bahrain with MCM packages). Trump posted Apr 8: 'The United States will be helping with the traffic buildup in the Strait of Hormuz. Big money will be made.' Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / IEA.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 244+ children (Iran) + UNICEF: 125+ (Lebanon) — ceasefire in effect — 3 boys lightly injured in post-ceasefire cluster munition hit on Beersheba — 15%+ of Iran casualties under 18
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran (15%+ of casualties under 18). UNICEF: 125+ children killed in Lebanon. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7. Post-ceasefire violations included cluster submunitions hitting Beersheba, lightly injuring 3 boys. Deadliest single incident: Minab girls' school (Feb 28) — 165-180 killed. WHO verified 23 attacks on healthcare facilities causing 9 deaths. 763 schools damaged. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / Amnesty International.
